rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture when inserting annex content; do not alter components, features, connections, or properties outside the annex addition.
Respect owner scope: type-level contracts belong on component types, while implementation-level bindings belong on implementations.
Do not move type-scope contract content into an implementation unless it is truly implementation-scoped.
Keep inserted content limited to valid annex blocks so surrounding structural declarations remain unchanged.
generic_rules: Match inserted content to the owning AADL element and preserve all existing declarations verbatim.
Avoid introducing references that are only valid in a different scope than the chosen owner.
Prefer minimal structural edits: add the needed annex content without reformatting or reorganizing unrelated model text.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-scoped AGREE content should appear in a valid implementation context, typically after relevant structural declarations if such dependencies exist.
anti_patterns: Do not insert type-scope port contracts into an implementation owner just because that is the current edit location.
Do not modify subcomponents, connections, feature declarations, or properties when the task is only annex insertion.
Do not preserve or introduce out-of-scope identifiers across owner boundaries.
irrelevant_topics: Temporal equation syntax and logical operator details
Scalar helper type declarations and expression typing rules
Requirement-specific assumptions or guarantees about particular signals
